title: The Peace-Offering
content:
It was but a little thing,
    Yet I knew it meant to me
    Ease from what had given a sting
    To the very birdsinging
     Latterly.

    But I would not welcome it;
    And for all I then declined
    O the regrettings infinite
    When the night-processions flit
     Through the mind!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Thomas Hardy, titled "The Peace-Offering", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
